def: s.f. Running off, flight ( = bhāgaṛ, q.v.). s.f. Flight, escape from immediate danger, general emigration; panic, stampede ( = bhāgā-bhāg; bhājaṛ).
s.f. Running off, flight ( = bhāgaṛ, q.v.). s.f. Flight, escape from immediate danger, general emigration; panic, stampede ( = bhāgā-bhāg; bhājaṛ).

def: s.f. Greens; (especially) fried or cooked greens (syn. sāg).

ety: bhāj˚ = Prk. भज्ज(इ) = S. भज्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. भंज्
def: v.n. To run away, flee, &c. ( = bhāgnā, q.v.). v.n. To run, to run off or away, fly, flee, take flight, escape, abscond, make oneself scarce
v.t. To fry, roast, grill, parch (syn. bhūnnā).

ety: Prk. भजणअं; S. भजनीयं, rt. भज्
def: v.t. To worship, &c. = bhajan karnā, q.v.s.v. bhajan. s.m. (in Arith.), Division; — vessel, pot, dish, plate (syn. bāsan).
v.n. = bhājnā; bhāgnā; qq.v. v.n. To run away, flee, &c. ( = bhāgnā, q.v.). v.n. To run, to run off or away, fly, flee, take flight, escape, abscond, make oneself scarce
adj. & s.m. To be divided, divisible, fit or requiring to be portioned or distributed; — portion, share, inheritance; (in Arith.) the dividend.
s.f. Puff, blast, flash (in the pan), explosion; — dry powdered tobacco (which is easily puffed or blown away)

v.n. To fly off with a flap or flop; to explode; to be cut clean off

def: s.m. Side, direction, &c. = bal, q.v. s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn

def: adj. Good, well, &c. = bhalā, q.v. (used only in comp.)

def: s.m. A large spear; lance ( = bhāl)

v.t. To take well, take in good part.

def: s.m. The marking-nut plant ( = bhilāwāṅ, q.v.). s.m. The marking-nut plant, Semecarpus anacardium (commonly called 'the Malacca bean'); the Acājon or cashew nut (from which is extracted an acid juice used for medicinal purposes, as well as a black liquid used for marking linen. It is also used for making wales on the body, which are shown in a criminal court as evidence of having been beaten).
s.f. A kind of arrow with a crescent-shaped head; the marking nut ( = bhallāt.)

s.m. Place where household goods and utensils are kept, store-room, store-house, depository, magazine, warehouse; the well of a native cart; receptacle for water, reservoir or tank; bed of a river; (fig.) head, skull; villages managed by a rājā or zamīndār himself; private estate (not rented or farmed to others); — bhanḍārā, s.m. Feast of Jogis, Sanyāsīs, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Fortunate, happy, prosperous, auspicious; — a messenger; the plant Mimosa sirisha.

adj. = bhanḍil, q.v. adj. & s.m. Fortunate, happy, prosperous, auspicious; — a messenger; the plant Mimosa sirisha.

def: s.f. Hemp, &c. = bhāṅg, q.v.
subs: s.m. The stick with which hemp-leaves are rubbed down to make the beverage called bhaṅg.

def: s.m. Breaking, splitting, breach, chasm, fissure; defeat, discomfiture, overthrow, rout; disappointment; interruption; breach of faith, infringement, violation; fraud; wave, breaker; the palsy.

wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھانجا (bhanja), from Prakrit 𑀪𑀸𑀬𑀡𑀺𑀚𑁆𑀚 (bhāyaṇijja) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit भागिनेय (bhāginēya).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Breaking, severing, destroying; afflicting; — breaker, destroyer.
adj. = bhanjak, q.v.; — s.m. Breaking, separating, dividing, demolishing destroying; violating, afflicting
v.t. To change money ( = bhunānā).
adj. Breaking, severing, destroying; — s.f. Discount, &c. = bhunāʼī, q.v.
s.f. Interruption, hindrance, prevention, obstruction; tale-bearing
subs: bhāṅjī khānā, bhāṅjī mārnā, v.n. To interfere, meddle; to interrupt, obstruct, break in (upon), put a stop (to); to thwart, frustrate; to give malicious intelligence, tell tales (of).
wety: Feminine of بھان٘جا (bhāñjā, “nephew”), from Prakrit 𑀪𑀸𑀬𑀡𑀺𑀚𑁆𑀚 (bhāyaṇijja) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit भागिनेय (bhāginēya).

def: s.f. Sister's daughter, niece.
wety: Feminine of بھان٘جا (bhāñjā, “nephew”), from Prakrit 𑀪𑀸𑀬𑀡𑀺𑀚𑁆𑀚 (bhāyaṇijja) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit भागिनेय (bhāginēya).

ety: S. भञ्जनीयं, rt. भञ्ज्; Prk. भंजणिअं
def: v.t. To break, destroy; — to put into circular motion; to turn on a lathe; to turn, twirl, twist, wind; to wave, brandish; to count one's beads; to repeat or recite (prayers, counting the beads the while).
v.n. To break, divide, part, go asunder, &c.; — v.t. To break, separate, sunder, &c. (see bhanjan).
s.m. String or cord formed by twisting together two or more threads, twisted cord or thread.
s.f. A jungle shrub, Ruella longifolia(?).
s.m. Fop, &c. ( = bāṅkā, bāṅkait); parasite.

ety: supposed to spring from the S. भानु, but prob. from S. भाण or भण्ड, with मत्
def: s.m. Actor; conjurer, juggler.
s.f. Actress; female juggler.

v.t. = bhāṅjnā, q.v. v.t. To break, destroy; — to put into circular motion; to turn on a lathe; to turn, twirl, twist, wind; to wave, brandish; to count one's beads; to repeat or recite (prayers, counting the beads the while).
v.n. To speak.
v.n. To sound, ring, buzz, hum, whiz; to have a ringing sound or buzzing (in the ears); to speak with a nasal twang; to get into a whirl; to be in fear (of).
v.t. = bhāphnā, q.v. v.t. To conceive; to guess; to comprehend; to make out, know, see through.
s.m. A knowing or astute person, a sharp fellow.
s.m. Jester, &c. = bhāṅḍ.
s.f. = bhaṛerī, bhanḍer, bhanḍolī.
s.m. = bhaṅḍerī, bhanḍeriyā, q.v. s.f. = bhanḍer, bhanḍolī, bhaṛerī.
s.f. = bhanḍsāl, q.v. s.f. Store-house, magazine, granary, — the provision which is previously reserved for years
s.m. = H بهنساري भनसारी bhansārī, s.f. Fire-place, &c. ( = bhaṛsāʼīṅ; bhāṛ).
s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Ahīr tribe.
s.f. = H بهنسار भनसार bhansār, s.m. Fire-place, &c. ( = bhaṛsāʼīṅ; bhāṛ).
v.n. = bhasnā, q.v. v.n. To be manifest, to appear, be known.
s.m. The medicinal plant Clerodeṅdron infortunatum (Linn.), or Volkameria infortunata (Roxb.).

s.m. = bhāṭā; bhaṅṭā, qq.v. s.m. = bhattā, q.v. s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest; ploughman's wages in kind; additional allowance, extra allowance or pay to public servants on special duty, or to officers and soldiers (commonly called 'batta'); subsistence money; travelling allowance
s.m. The egg-plant, Solanum melongena (syn. baiṅgan).
s.m. Ploughman's wages in kind (see bhāt, bhātā, bhattā).

s.f. Way, manner, mode, method, style, fashion; variety, kind, sort, class; — bhāṅṭ-bhāṅṭ or bhāṅti-bhāṅti (-ke), adj. Various kinds (of), various, diverse, of many kinds or sorts, miscellaneous, multifarious
subs: bhalī-bhāṅt, adv. Well, thoroughly, fully, quite, soundly, severely, &c.
kisī bhāṅti adv. By some means

def: s.m. The fruit of the koʼī or water-lily.
s.m. = biṭmās, q.v. s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
s.m. The fruit of the koʼī or water-lily.
s.m. = biṭmās, q.v. s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
s.m. = بهان bhānu, q.v. s.m. Appearance, brightness, light; ray of light; the sun; a planet; any luminary; master; sovereign
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. bhānu-matī), Luminous, splendid, resplendent; beautiful, handsome; — the sun; a handsome person
s.f. A beautiful or handsome woman

def: s.m. Appearance, brightness, light; ray of light; the sun; a planet; any luminary; master; sovereign
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. bhānu-matī), Luminous, splendid, resplendent; beautiful, handsome; — the sun; a handsome person
s.f. A beautiful or handsome woman

def: s.f. See s.v. bhānu. s.m. Appearance, brightness, light; ray of light; the sun; a planet; any luminary; master; sovereign
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. bhānu-matī), Luminous, splendid, resplendent; beautiful, handsome; — the sun; a handsome person
s.f. A beautiful or handsome woman

def: s.m. Name of a small class of Rājpūts.
s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
subs: bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.m. Revolution, gyration, whirl; whirlpool, eddy, vortex (syn. gird-āb); a large black bee, the humble bee; creeping plant, creeper
subs: s.m. (Met.), The world and its snares
(S. bhramara+kaṭa+ikā), s.f. A turning or movable ring attached to a rope (for confining an animal or thing); collar or halter (for a dog, goat, &c.); a swivel
s.m.f. The songs which the cowherdesses of Mathurā addressed to the bees
v.n. To whirl round, revolve; to get into a whirl, &c.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھنور (bhnvr /⁠bhãvar⁠/) (c. 1658), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀪𑀫𑀭 (bhamara), from Sanskrit भ्रमर (bhramará, “large black bee; spinning top”).

def: v.n. To go round, &c. = bhāṅwar phirnā, q.v.s.v. bhāṅwar. s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
subs: bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.f. = bhaṅbīrī; bhaṅwrī, qq.v. v.n. To run swiftly (said of a child).

s.m. A large black bee, &c. ( = bhaṅwar and bhauṅrā, qq.v.). s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
subs: bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.f. = bhāṅwar, q.v.
subs: v.n. = bhāṅwar phirnā, q.v. s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.f. dim. of bhaṅwrā, q.v.
s.f. Rent paid to a zamīndār in kind (usually to the extent of half the produce); distribution of the crop between the landlord and the cultivator
subs: s.m. Land that has long been cultivated
s.m. Land recently brought under cultivation.

def: postp. = bhāveṅ, q.v. = S بهاوي भावे bhāwe, postp. In the sentiment, perception, mind, notion or idea (of, -ke); in consideration (of); with regard (to); concerning, for; with a view (to), for the purpose (of), in order that; in the manner (of), after the fashion (of), like (as); in the eyes (of), near, before, to
v.n. To revolve, to whirl ( = bhamnā; bhaunā).
v.n. = bhānā, q.v. v.n. To be approved (of), to be acceptable (to, -ko), be pleasing (to), to please; to be beloved, be held dear; to suit, fit, become; to seem good or befitting (syn. aććhā lagnā).
adj. = bhāvī, q.v. s.m. Future time, the future
subs: adj. Dependent on, or subject to, the future.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Amiable, dear, loved; — beloved, love.
s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; hot breath ( = bāph)
subs: v.t. To feed its young with its bill (a pigeon).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھاپھہ (bhaphh /⁠bhāph⁠/) (c. 1419) and later as بھاپ (bhap /⁠bhāp⁠/), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑀧𑁆𑀨 (bappha), from Sanskrit वाष्प (vāṣpa, “tear”).

def: v.n. = bhabaknā, q.v. v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
s.m. Breath; wheedling, deception, deceit, fraud (syn. dam).
s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; hot breath ( = bāph)
subs: v.t. To feed its young with its bill (a pigeon).

def: s.m. = bhapārā, q.v. s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
v.t. To conceive; to guess; to comprehend; to make out, know, see through.
s.m. = bhabkā, q.v. s.m. Blast from a furnace; alembic, still; — a kind of drinking vessel with a large mouth.
s.m. = bhapārā, q.v. s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
v.t. To conceive; to guess; to comprehend; to make out, know, see through.
s.m. = phapolā, q.v. s.m. A blister, vesicle; a bubble
subs: v.n. To be blistered; (met.) to be afflicted in mind
v.n. To satisfy a revenge that has long been rankling in one's breast; to give vent to one's passion, appease one's wrath.

def: s.m. = bhapāṛā, q.v. s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
s.m. Load, burden ( = bojh); weight, gravity; a weight of gold equal to 2,000 palas; fagot; yoke for carrying burdens; charge, trust, responsibility
subs: (-kā), To put down a load or burden; to throw off the responsibility (of); to deliver (from), to redeem, make atonement (for)
s.m. A cart ( = P. bār-kash); the strap which fastens a horse, &c. to the shafts or pole of a carriage, or cart, &c.
adj. & s.m. Weighted, loaded, burdened, oppressed; — an oppressed person, &c.
bhār-vāhik, s.m. Bearer of burdens, porter, carrier (syn. moṭiyā; kahār)
(vulg. bhār-br̤iksh), s.m. A fragrant substance (commonly called kākshī), considered variously as a vegetable and a mineral product.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَهَار (bahār, “spring”). Doublet of وَسَنْت (vasant) and بَسَنْت (basant).

def: adj. Corr. of bāhir or bāhar, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَهَار (bahār, “spring”). Doublet of وَسَنْت (vasant) and بَسَنْت (basant).

def: s.m. Oven, furnace, kiln, fire-place for parching grain
subs: v.n. To light or heat the oven; to make a fire of sticks, leaves, &c. for parching grain, &c.; to put fuel on the fire, feed the fire; (fig.) to take up a mean occupation
bhāṛ-meṅ ḍālnā, v.t. To throw into the fire; to fling away, cast to the dogs.

def: s.m. Wages of prostitution; price of fornication
subs: v.n. To live on the wages of prostitution (not the woman, but her dependants); — s.m. A pimp, &c. ( = bhaṛuʼā, q.v.).

def: s.m. Load, burden; plenty, size, bulk
subs: v.n. To be paid in full, to receive the amount due; to be paid off, get one's deserts
s.f. Receipt in full (the name being derived from the use of these words in the body of the receipt)
bhar-pūraṇ, adj. Quite full, brimful, chokeful, crammed, replete, overflowing; sufficient, quite enough; high (as a tide); full (as the moon)
s.f. The state of being full or overflowing, fullness; abundance
adv. In full of all demands
adv. At full gallop; — to the extent of one's power, &c. = bhar-śak
bhar-śak, adv. To the extent of one's ability, as far as possible, as far as may be; with all one's might
s.m. A full kos; fully a kos
adv. Such a long time, all that while
adv. About a ser; a ser
adv. About a kos; a kos (cf. bhar-kos)

def: s.m. Name of one of the aboriginal races of India
subs: s.m. A subdivision of the bhar tribe.

def: s.m. Large boat, barge, lighter.

s.m. Sound of crackling fuel, crackle; crash; roll (of musketry); rat-tat; rush, whir; sound of breaking wind; — bhaṛ-bhaṛ, s.m. = bhaṛ (and is the more common form); adv. With a crash, or gush or rush, &c.
subs: v.n. To burn fiercely; to roar, crackle, crack, crash, &c.

def: s.m. Load, burden ( = bhār); throwing any burden or responsibility on a person; — scaffolding.
s.m. Hire, fare, freight, carriage, rent; price, recompense
subs: s.m. Hired pony; hack; (fig.) the slave of necessity or habit.

def: s.m. A kind of grass ( = bhaḍā: cf. bharī).
part. adj. Filled, filled with, full, full of, replete, brimful, overflowing (com. used at the end of comp., e.g. mal-bharā, adj. Replete with filth
subs: adj. Charged with venom); — s.m. Filing; fulness; contents, load, cargo, charge (of a gun)
adj. Wealthy, prosperous, flourishing, thriving, fortunate, happy; having in abundance; abounding in; having a large family.

def: s.m. A skylark; wild cotton; a bone; — a proper name.
s.m. lit. 'Bringing or bearing food'; the skylark (com. called bhart).
s.f. Filling, stuffing ( = bharāwaṭ); — price paid for filling or stuffing.
s.f. A cess or tax (in the Benares district), one half of which is given to the ʻāmil for charges of remittance, and the other carried to the credit of government.
s.m. = bhaṛait, q.v.
s.m. = bhaṛaitī, q.v. s.m. Tenant, lessee
s.m. Tenant, lessee
s.m. (local), Fruit-garden, orchard.

ety: S. स्फर = स्फुर+क; cf. also S. भर्ग
def: s.f. Splendour, glitter, flash, blaze; show, pomp, parade, pageantry; burst, explosion; flame; burst of anger, paroxysm, rage, fury; perturbation, agitation, alarm; starting; shying (of a horse); start, shy; quivering, throb, pulsation, twitch, &c.
subs: adj. Splendid, shining, glittering, blazing, refulgent.

def: v.n. To start, shrink, be scared or alarmed; to shy (a horse); to throb violently, beat, palpitate, quiver, twitch (as the eyelid, &c.); to break or burst forth into flame, blaze up, break out; to take fire; to fly into a passion, become very excited; to be over-heated; to break, crack (as a vessel); to be dislocated, be displaced.

ety: S. धर्म, by change of initial consonant; though supposed by some to come from S. भ्रम
def: s.m. Character, reputation, esteem, credit
subs: v.t. To gain a reputation; to establish (one's) credit
v.t. To credit, believe, rely upon, depend on
v.t. To sacrifice (one's) reputation or character, to lose (one's) credit.

def: s.m. The act of carrying, bearing, supporting, maintaining, sustaining, nourishing; filling, satisfying; paying in full; wages, hire; — bharan, s.f. Heavy shower of rain; — postp. To, up to, till ( = tak)
subs: s.m. Supply of food and raiment, maintenance.

def: v.t. To cause to be full; to cause to fill; to fill; to feed its young (a bird); to have (a mare) covered.
adj. = bhaṛbhaṛiyā, q.v.
s.f. The second Nakshatra or lunar mansion (containing three stars in Musca, and figured by the pudendum muliebre); — weft, woof; a shuttle.
s.f. Corn remaining in the ear after it has been trodden or threshed (syn. gāṅṭhā).

s.m. Conjuror, &c. = bhanḍeriyā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Appertaining to conjuring, fortune-telling, &c.; conjuring, fortune-telling; — a tribe or caste who are conjurers, fortune-tellers, &c. (they form a subdivision of the tribe called ḍakaut, q.v.); a member of this tribe, conjurer; fortune-teller, &c.

adj. & s.m. Descended from Bharat; — a descendant of Bharat; the great epic of the Hindūs (containing the account of Yudhisthir's war, and usually called the Mahā-bhārat); name of India Proper (so called because it was the patrimony of Bharat, son of Dushyant, who is said to have been the first monarch of all India)
s.m. Name of a younger brother of Rāma, and son of Dasharath by Kaikeyī; name of several princes; also of a certain sage, the inventor of dramatic composition; — actor, mimic; weaver
subs: s.m. Name of a city and district in Northern India
s.m. Name of a tribe of Brāhmans
s.m. One of the nine divisions of the world, India (from Ceylon to Tartary)

def: s.m. Filling; stuffing, insertion, padding; room, space, place; vessel, bag, &c.; goods, cargo, lading, freight; fare, carriage, conveyance, transit or transport charges; amount paid in; amount of revenue paid by an individual or party; payment in full
subs: (-meṅ), To put in the stuffing, &c.; — to fill, stuff, pad; to make up the full tale or quantity, supply a deficiency; to convey merchandize (to a place), to export.

def: s.m. Potter; servant.
s.m. Loader; trader, merchant.
s.m. Load of wood (cf. bharoṭā).
s.f. Release in full; receipt in acknowledgment of full payment; acknowledgment; indemnity.
s.f. Filling, stuffing; anything in which a substance is filled (see bhartī).
s.m. A kind of prickly poppy, Argemone mexicana (cf. ūṅṭ-kaṭāra).
s.m. = bhaṛ; and bhabbhar, qq.v. s.m. Load, burden ( = bojh); weight, gravity; a weight of gold equal to 2,000 palas; fagot; yoke for carrying burdens; charge, trust, responsibility
subs: (-kā), To put down a load or burden; to throw off the responsibility (of); to deliver (from), to redeem, make atonement (for)
s.m. A cart ( = P. bār-kash); the strap which fastens a horse, &c. to the shafts or pole of a carriage, or cart, &c.
adj. & s.m. Weighted, loaded, burdened, oppressed; — an oppressed person, &c.
bhār-vāhik, s.m. Bearer of burdens, porter, carrier (syn. moṭiyā; kahār)
(vulg. bhār-br̤iksh), s.m. A fragrant substance (commonly called kākshī), considered variously as a vegetable and a mineral product.

def: s.f. Swelling, inflammation.

ety: redupl. of S. भर, rt. भृ
def: To swell and be glossy, to be inflamed (as the skin in fever).

v.n. To crackle, &c. ( = bhaṛ-bhaṛ karnā, q.v.s.v. bhaṛ); to flutter. s.m. = bhaṛ; and bhabbhar, qq.v. s.m. Load, burden ( = bojh); weight, gravity; a weight of gold equal to 2,000 palas; fagot; yoke for carrying burdens; charge, trust, responsibility
subs: (-kā), To put down a load or burden; to throw off the responsibility (of); to deliver (from), to redeem, make atonement (for)
s.m. A cart ( = P. bār-kash); the strap which fastens a horse, &c. to the shafts or pole of a carriage, or cart, &c.
adj. & s.m. Weighted, loaded, burdened, oppressed; — an oppressed person, &c.
bhār-vāhik, s.m. Bearer of burdens, porter, carrier (syn. moṭiyā; kahār)
(vulg. bhār-br̤iksh), s.m. A fragrant substance (commonly called kākshī), considered variously as a vegetable and a mineral product.

def: s.f. Swelling, inflammation.
s.f. Fierce blazing of fire; loud, deep, and confused sound; rolling, roaring, rattling, pealing, bellowing (as of guns, of a storm, &c.: see bhaṛ-bhaṛ).
s.f. = bharbharāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Swelling, inflammation.

adj. & s.m. Speaking from the impulse of the moment; blurting out without consideration whatever comes uppermost; simple, guileless, artless; plain-spoken, candid; — one who blurts out whatever comes uppermost, &c.; a simple, or guileless person, &c.; — prater, chatterbox, babbler ( = baṛ baṛiyā).
s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose occupation is to parch grain; a member of this caste, a grain-parcher.
s.f. Woman of the bhaṛbhūṅjā caste; wife of a bhaṛbhūṅjā; a woman who parches grain.
adj. & s.m. = bhaṅḍerī; bhaṅṛerī, qq.v. s.f. = bhanḍer, bhanḍolī, bhaṛerī.
s.f. = bhanḍer; bhaṅṛerī, qq.v. s.f. A row or pile of water-pots one above the other (syn. bhaṛerī, bhanḍer, bhanḍerī).

adj. Heavy, weighty, ponderous, massive, unwieldy; large, big, bulky; great, grand; crowded (as a meeting or fair); swollen, inflamed; of importance, important, momentous, grave, serious; valuable, costly; patient; difficult, hard, laborious, burdensome, troublesome, grievous, oppressive, unsupportable, insufferable, distressing; dull, dun, dark (as colour); heavy-footed, slow, sluggish (of pace); hoarse, deep, bass (voice); hard-of-hearing; loud-sounding; sad, dull, dejected (in mind or spirits), languid; — s.m. Carrier of burdens, porter, &c.
subs: s.f. Hoarse, or deep, sound or tone; bass or deep voice
adj. Grave, serious, solemn; sedate; patient, long-suffering
To withdraw from a difficult or impracticable undertaking
v.n. To weigh heavily (upon); to feel the burden (of); to be heavy, tedious, or tiresome
To be heavy, or burdensome; to feel the burden (of), be weary (of); to be heavy, dull, dejected; to weigh heavier (than, -par), to outweigh.

def: s.f. The weight of one sicca rupee (i.e. one tolā or twelve māshās).
s.f. A jungle grass (which grows to the height of nine feet, and is used for thatching, &c.).
s.m. Heaviness, weightiness, weight.
part. Filled; full ( = bharā, q.v.). s.m. Load, burden ( = bhār); throwing any burden or responsibility on a person; — scaffolding.
s.m. Land watered by irrigation.
s.f. = bhāryā, q.v. s.f. Wife, spouse; married woman; a musical mode; the wife of a rāg or deity presiding over a musical mode
subs: s.m. A husband under the rule of his wife, a hen-pecked husband.

def: s.m. The actof roasting, baking, frying, parching, or scorching (syn. bhūnnā).
s.m. Slaked lime.
v.t. To slake lime.
v.t. To startle, scare, frighten; to blow up into a flame, to kindle, inflame, excite, instigate, &c. (see bhaṛaknā).
adj. Splendid, glittering, &c. = bhaṛak-dār and bhaṛkīlā, qq.v.
adv. In full; to the full.
adj. Splendid, glittering, gorgeous, showy, flashy, gaudy; shy, skittish, coy; untamed, wild; timid.
